fm2026Details: Magnus force model with perpendicular velocity vectors. Spin stored as ball.cvy (lateral) and ball.cvx (topspin). Curl force: spinFactor * (speed / 15.0) applied perpendicular to velocity direction every physics tick on ALL free balls (shots, passes, crosses, clearances). Topspin calculated from technique stat (>65 → topspin = (technique-60)/4.0). Spin dampens 95% per bounce (SPIN_DAMPING_GROUND=0.95). Constants: SPIN_FORCE_FACTOR=1.2 (updated from 0.8), AIR_DRAG_COEFF=0.015. calculateCurl() generates curl components from technique/curve/shooting/crossing stats and preferred foot. CONFIRMED: Curl is NOT limited to free kicks — it applies to any ball with spin components. Additional: ball dribble pivot at 12.0 rad/s for smooth direction changes.
legacyDetails: Polynomial equation-based curl. Curl stored as m_CurlVelocity (IntVector3). Formula: equation = 1 + (((v*v) * 0.5f) - (v * 1.5f)). Applied as: posx += (int)(equation * (pCVel.x * 0.003f)). Curl zeroed after bounce. Fixed curl divisor of 65. Constants: AIR_FRICTION=0.0002, curlamount=65.
gapAnalysis:
Different approaches: FM2026 uses physics-based Magnus force (simpler, more realistic), Legacy uses polynomial frame-dependent calculation (more complex, less physical). FM2026 adds topspin/backspin (Legacy has no vertical curl). FM2026 curl scales dynamically with ball speed; Legacy uses fixed divisor. FM2026 retains 95% spin per bounce; Legacy zeroes spin entirely on bounce. FM2026 applies curl to ALL free balls continuously. SPIN_FORCE_FACTOR increased to 1.2 (was 0.8) in 17 Feb update.

FM2026 exceeds Legacy here. No gap.
codeSuggestions: No fix needed — FM2026's curl model is physically more accurate than Legacy's polynomial approach.

fm2026Details: Post collision: 4 posts with FIFA geometry (52.5m, ±3.66m). Distance-based detection (BALL_RADIUS 0.11 + POST_RADIUS 0.06 = 0.17m). Vector reflection with restitution=0.6. Crossbar: 3 modes (under 50% bounce, over 70% bounce, face 40% reflection). Net: 4-zone damping system — back net 95% X velocity kill, side net 75% Y kill, top net 85% vertical cull, general NET_DAMPING=12.0. Emits BALL_COLLISION events. Goal detection inside-goal zone.
legacyDetails: Post collision: stamped debounce (200 frames). Angle-based deflection with deflectiondiff normalized to HALF_BALL_SIZE. X velocity reduced by factor z, Y/Z halved. Crossbar: height-based deflection, upper/lower bar calculations. Netting: back net 95% X cull, side net 75% Z cull, top net variants. Uses p=1-6 collision type identifiers.
gapAnalysis:
FM2026 has modern physics-based collision (vector reflection, restitution coefficient). Legacy uses angle-based reduction with arbitrary multipliers. FM2026 adds explicit net damping zone (Legacy has separate net collision types). FM2026 uses continuous collision detection; Legacy uses stamped debounce.

FM2026 is MORE COMPLETE here — it has post, crossbar, AND net detection. Legacy has more granular net collision types (back/side/top separately) but FM2026's approach is cleaner.
codeSuggestions: Consider adding separate net collision zones (back net vs side net vs top net) for more realistic ball behavior in the net, similar to Legacy's p=1,2,3,4 system. Currently FM2026 treats the entire goal zone with uniform damping.

fm2026Details: [RESOLVED 11 Feb] New ballPrediction.js (131 lines) with 3 static methods: predictPosition(ball, futureTime, dt=0.05) — full Euler simulation with gravity -9.81, quadratic air drag, bounce damping 0.65, ground decel 6.5. timeToReachHeight(ball, targetHeight, maxTime=3.0) — binary search (15 iterations). findInterceptPoint(ball, playerPos, playerSpeed) — iterative search with 0.3m reach margin, 3.5s lookahead. Used by aiKeeper for high ball claiming, sweep interception, and shot arrival prediction. Used by playerAIController for loose ball chase. Missing: spin/Magnus effect, collision prediction with players, next-bounce prediction.
legacyDetails: Explicit quadratic trajectory prediction. NewTimeBallWillBeClosest(): linear projection with 500-unit lookahead, dot-product based closest point calculation. NewTimeBallWillBeHeadable(): peak height calculation PHA=((vel.y/200f)+0.1f)*vel.y, with 10-element BallDropTable lookup. Time-to-ground: complex iterative calculation (max 2500 iterations) with flight percentage tracking.
gapAnalysis: [RESOLVED 11 Feb] Ball prediction now implemented in ballPrediction.js covering ~85-90% of legacy capability. Both engines now have intercept calculation, height-crossing detection, and trajectory prediction. Remaining minor gaps: no spin in prediction, no collision prediction with players, no next-bounce prediction.
codeSuggestions: RESOLVED — ballPrediction.js implements predictPosition(), timeToReachHeight(), findInterceptPoint(). Remaining improvement: integrate SPIN_FORCE_FACTOR into prediction for curved ball paths.

fm2026Details: Bounce detection at height<=0.11m. Vertical restitution: 65% (BOUNCE_DAMPING=0.65). Curl-to-velocity transfer: 1.5x multiplier. Spin zeroed after bounce. Ground deceleration: 9.0 m/s² (GROUND_DECEL). Stop threshold: |vh|<0.5 or planarSpeed<0.5 (ROLL_STOP_SPEED).
legacyDetails: Vertical restitution: 50% (GROUND_BOUNCE=0.5). Horizontal friction: 20% (GROUND_BOUNCE_FRICTION=0.2). Curl-to-velocity transfer: 10x multiplier. Spin zeroed after bounce. Rolling friction: frame-dependent (GROUND_FRICTION=10 * frame * 0.5). Stop threshold: speed < GRAVITY/2.
gapAnalysis:
Both have complete bounce physics with different parameters. FM2026 is MORE bouncy (65% vs 50% retention) but has less dramatic spin transfer (1.5x vs 10x). FM2026 uses constant deceleration (9 m/s²) which is more physically realistic than Legacy's frame-dependent friction.

No critical gap — different but both functional. FM2026 arguably more realistic.
codeSuggestions: Consider increasing curl-to-velocity transfer from 1.5x to something closer to 3-5x if balls feel too 'flat' after bouncing. The Legacy 10x is excessive but the current 1.5x may be too conservative for noticeable spin effects.

fm2026Details: [UPDATED 3 Mar] Upgrade engine SIGNIFICANTLY REWORKED. Four-stage upgradeInternal(): (1) Weighted stat selection pool — role-priority stats get 3x weight, stats below 40 get 2x catch-up bonus, stats above 90 get 0.2x resistance. (2) Stat count by rarity: Regular 1-2, Rare 2-3, Epic 3-4, Legendary 4-5. (3) Sacrifice bonus pool — multiplier by sacrifice rarity (0.05-0.25) * 0.8 + sacrifice abilityScore fraction. (4) Diminishing returns: pow(1-current/maxVal, 0.7) — stat 0 = 1.0x gain, stat 90 = 0.15x gain, minimum gain 1. Post-upgrade: rarity recalculated via getQualityFromScore(), stars/starPercent recalculated, NFT metadata updated on-chain if rarity changed. Stat pool init order fixed (3 Mar commit). Heal: BoosterType.Heal, clears injury to 0, fatigue recovery by rarity (Regular 2, Legendary 25). Client: PlayerUpgradeResultPopup — animated stat comparison with value counters (3s lerp), rarity transition animation (fade old→fade in new), 'Upgrade Again' button loops back. SHOT DECISION: State-machine driven — player enters Shooting() state via tactical AI. MaxDist: 18+(PowerStat/5), modified by flags (fromdistance+8, shootonsight+2). No GK awareness — shoots at goal center. No free kick or penalty strategy.

SHOT EXECUTION: Power=25000+(75*ShootingStat). Volley boost +50 if height>0.1m. Accuracy penalty: (100-AccuracyStat)*5 with 4x random deviation (X-axis 2x more scatter than Z). Height varies by ball position: ground shots 1500-3000, volleys ±5000-7500. Keeper jink (chip): halves power, Y=7000.

GK REACTION: KeeperActions.cs — ball trajectory predicted via NewTimeBallWillBeClosest(). Brain delay: 200ms+(distance*10ms). 12 discrete dive types: 3 heights (Low/Mid/High) x 2 directions x 2 distances (Close/Far). Dive distance: 2m+(ShotStoppingStat/120)*1.67m. Rush distance: 1.5m+(FlairStat/100)*1m.

SAVE MECHANICS: Animation-driven timing — StrikeStamp determines catch window (150-578ms). If StrikeStamp<GameTime: must parry, else catch. Parry direction: HandlingStat controls predictability. ParryBall(): speed reduced by ShotStoppingStat/20, random redirect on low HandlingStat. Miss chance: random(0,100)>ShotStoppingStat.

GOAL DETECTION: Ball.x past GOALLINE+HALF_BALL_SIZE, within POST_DISTANCE, below BAR_HEIGHT. Post/bar collision via Collisions.cs with deflection angles.

legacyDetails:
Legacy GK catch system is timing-based, not probability-based. If StrikeStamp > GameTime (keeper reacted in time), the keeper CATCHES the ball — attack ends. If StrikeStamp < GameTime (too late), the keeper PARRIES. Parry quality: speed reduced by ShotStoppingStat/20, with random redirect on low HandlingStat. Miss chance: random(0,100) > ShotStoppingStat — so a keeper with 80 ShotStopping misses 20% of parry attempts.

Critically, Legacy's parry is a LAST RESORT when the keeper is too late, not the DEFAULT outcome. Most saves in Legacy are catches that cleanly end the attack.

codeSuggestions:
COMPREHENSIVE REBALANCE: 7 areas of change to achieve realistic scorelines (0-0 through 5-0 range, average ~1.4 goals/team) in a full 90-minute real-time simulation. Apply ALL areas together for best results — they are interdependent.

========================================
AREA 1: SLOW DOWN DECISION CADENCE
========================================
This is the single biggest lever. Players currently think 2x per second — they should think every 2-4 seconds during build-up.

File: playerStateController.js (lines 147-160)
Current:
  const base = 0.35 + (1 - vision / 100) * 0.40;  // 0.35-0.75s
  const variance = 0.15 + (1 - composure / 100) * 0.25;  // 0.15-0.40s
  return Math.max(0.50, base + randomOffset);

Suggested:
  const base = 1.5 + (1 - vision / 100) * 1.5;  // 1.5-3.0s
  const variance = 0.3 + (1 - composure / 100) * 0.7;  // 0.3-1.0s
  return Math.max(1.5, base + randomOffset);

Rationale: A 1.5s minimum (up from 0.5s) means players think every 1.5-4.0s. Over 5,400s, this reduces total decisions from ~10,800 to ~2,700 per player — matching real football tempo where a player touches the ball 40-60 times per match.

IMPORTANT: Consider adding a ZONE-BASED modifier — faster decisions in the final third (1.0s-2.0s) and slower in build-up (2.0s-4.0s). This creates realistic tempo acceleration near goal:
  const zoneFactor = isInFinalThird ? 0.65 : (isInOwnHalf ? 1.3 : 1.0);
  return Math.max(1.0, (base + randomOffset) * zoneFactor);

========================================
AREA 2: INCREASE BALL CONTROL AND TRANSITION TIMES
========================================

--- 2A: Longer control phase ---
File: playerAIController.js (lines 608-628)
Current:
  const controlTimeNeeded = Math.max(0.2, 0.8 - (controlAttr / 200));

Suggested:
  const controlTimeNeeded = Math.max(0.8, 2.0 - (controlAttr / 100));

New range: 0.8s (control=100+) to 2.0s (control=0). Average player (control=65): ~1.35s.
This simulates the time to trap, settle, and look up before deciding.

--- 2B: Longer action cooldowns ---
File: playerEngine.js
Current pass cooldown (line 81): 0.8s → Change to: 1.8s
Current shot cooldown (line 133): 1.5s → Change to: 3.5s
Current dribble cooldown (line 157): 1.2s → Change to: 2.5s

Rationale: After playing a pass, a player shouldn't make another decision for ~2 seconds (jog into space, reposition). After shooting, ~3.5 seconds (recovery, getting back in play). Combined with the 1.5-4s decision interval, each player touches the ball every 3-6 seconds — matching real football.

--- 2C: Longer possession time limit ---
File: playerAIController.js (DRIBBLE.POSSESSION_TIME_LIMIT)
Current: 2.5s → Change to: 5.0s
Rationale: Allows more build-up dribbling and holding play. 2.5s forces panic passing.

--- 2D: Increase hesitation ---
File: playerAIController.js (lines 630-651)
Current:
  const hesitationChance = clamp((0.3 - (compositeSkill / 100 * 0.3)) * fatigueImpact, 0.02, 0.5);

Suggested:
  const hesitationChance = clamp((0.5 - (compositeSkill / 100 * 0.35)) * fatigueImpact, 0.08, 0.6);

New range: 8% (elite) to 60% (low skill). This adds realistic pauses — even world-class players don't instantly execute every time they receive.

========================================
AREA 3: STRENGTHEN DEFENSIVE EFFECTIVENESS
========================================

--- 3A: Larger interception radius ---
File: gameEngine.js (_checkBallPickup, around line 280)
Current:
  const baseRadius = 0.65;
  const speedBonus = Math.max(0, 1 - planarSpeed / 12) * 0.6;

Suggested:
  const baseRadius = 1.4;
  const speedBonus = Math.max(0, 1 - planarSpeed / 18) * 1.0;

New range: 1.4m (fast ball) to 2.4m (slow ball). This means defenders in the passing lane actually intercept passes rather than watching the ball zip past within arm's reach. The increase from 0.65m to 1.4m reflects a real player's ability to stick out a leg, chest-trap, or head an interception.

--- 3B: Wider challenge trigger range ---
File: challengeController.js (around line 124)
Current: challenge triggers within 3.0m
Suggested: increase to 4.5m
Rationale: A defender 4m away can lunge, extend a leg, or body-block. 3.0m is too tight.

--- 3C: Wider tackle commitment distance ---
File: aiPress.js (lines 214-238)
Current: 1.4m commitment to tackle
Suggested: 2.2m
Rationale: Slide tackles cover 2-3m in real football. Standing tackles reach ~1.5-2m with leg extension.

--- 3D: Faster defensive repositioning ---
File: playerEngine.js (around line 318)
Current: tactical positioning speed = 2.2 m/s (walking)
Suggested: 3.5 m/s (jogging) when ball is in own half, 2.8 m/s otherwise
  const defSpeed = ballInOwnHalf ? 3.5 : 2.8;
Rationale: Defenders jog to position, they don't walk. 2.2 m/s lets attackers sprint past repositioning defenders every time.

--- 3E: Reduce transition lag ---
File: supportController.js (around line 145)
Current: 0.9s formation freeze after turnover
Suggested: 0.4s
Rationale: Real defenders start tracking runs within 1-2 steps of losing the ball. 0.9s at sprint speed = 5m+ of free running for attackers, which is an entire counter-attack.

========================================
AREA 4: IMPROVE PRESSING AND MARKING
========================================

--- 4A: Increase press trigger distance ---
File: aiPress.js (PRESS_TRIGGER_DISTANCE)
Current: 12m → Suggested: 20m
Rationale: Modern pressing starts much further out. Liverpool's press triggers at 25-30m from the ball carrier in real life. 12m means only engaging when the attacker is already dangerously close.

--- 4B: Allow 3 players to press ---
File: aiPress.js (line 177)
Current: myRank <= 1 (max 2 pressers)
Suggested: myRank <= 2 (max 3 pressers)
Rationale: Real defensive pressing involves a ball-side triangle — one player presses, two cover passing lanes. This dramatically reduces easy passing options.

--- 4C: Fix defender marking past midline ---
File: aiOffBall.js (lines 518-520)
Current: defenders completely stop man-marking when ball is in opponent half
Suggested: defenders in own half should ALWAYS track nearby attackers, regardless of ball position. Only defenders who have pushed up past midfield should drop marking:
  const shouldMark = !pastMidline || (player.pos.x * attackDir < 0);  // Always mark if in own half
Rationale: The current behaviour means on any turnover, attackers in the defensive half are completely unmarked for the first 1-2 seconds. This is the single biggest exploit for counter-attacks.

========================================
AREA 5: INCREASE SHOOTING DIFFICULTY
========================================

--- 5A: Raise shot thresholds ---
File: aiConstants.js (lines 9-10)
Current:
  THRESHOLD_CLOSE: 0.72
  THRESHOLD_DISTANT: 0.82

Suggested:
  THRESHOLD_CLOSE: 0.82
  THRESHOLD_DISTANT: 0.92

Rationale: With the 1.44x close-range boost, the current 0.72 threshold is reached from a raw score of just 0.50 inside the box. That's too easy — half the time a player faces the goal inside the box, they shoot. Raising to 0.82 means they need a raw score of 0.57+, requiring good angle AND low pressure.

--- 5B: Increase shot inaccuracy ---
File: aiConstants.js (SHOT_INACCURACY_MULTIPLIER)
Current: 4.0 → Suggested: 7.0
Rationale: Target on-target rate is 33-38%. With 4x, too many shots still find the target over 5,400 seconds. 7x means more shots sail wide or over the bar, matching real football where ~62-67% of shots miss the target entirely.

--- 5C: Widen shot aim error range ---
File: aiShot.js (around lines 185-220)
Current:
  BASE_ERROR_MIN: 8.0 (shooting=100)
  BASE_ERROR_MAX: 28.0 (shooting=0)

Suggested:
  BASE_ERROR_MIN: 12.0
  BASE_ERROR_MAX: 40.0
Rationale: Even elite strikers miss the target ~45% of the time in real football. The wider error range creates more realistic shot scatter.

--- 5D: Add team-wide shot cooldown ---
File: playerAIController.js or gameEngine.js
New mechanic: After any player on a team shoots, suppress all shot scores for that team by 50% for 15 seconds.
  if (matchState.lastShotTime[teamIdx] && (now - matchState.lastShotTime[teamIdx]) < 15.0) {
      shotScore *= 0.5;
  }
Rationale: Real football teams don't shoot every time they enter the box. After a shot, play typically resets — goal kick, corner, or the other team clears. A 15-second suppression creates natural gaps between shots matching real football rhythm of ~1 shot every 6-7 minutes per team.

--- 5E: Reduce close-range stacking boosts ---
File: playerAIController.js (around the shot boost section)
Current boosts: 1.05x at 25m, 1.15x at 16m, 1.25x at 10m (stacks to ~1.44x)
Suggested: 1.03x at 25m, 1.08x at 16m, 1.12x at 10m (stacks to ~1.24x)
Rationale: Current 1.44x makes the 0.72 threshold effectively 0.50 at point blank. With the new 0.82 threshold and 1.24x boost, the effective threshold inside the box is 0.66, still requiring decent angle and low pressure.

========================================
AREA 6: STRENGTHEN GOALKEEPER
========================================

--- 6A: Faster GK reaction time ---
File: keeperAction.js (reaction delay calculation)
Current: 0.03 * (1.5 - agility/200 - confidence/200) = 0.015-0.045s
Suggested: 0.02 * (1.5 - agility/150 - confidence/150) = 0.007-0.030s
Rationale: GKs need to be slightly faster to react over more shots across 90 minutes. The cumulative effect matters.

--- 6B: Increase GK catch radius ---
File: physicsConstants.js
Current GK_CATCH_RADIUS: 3.0m → Suggested: 3.5m
Current GK_PARRY_RADIUS: 4.5m → Suggested: 5.5m
Rationale: GKs should cover more of the goal. This increases save rate toward the target 72%.

--- 6C: Improve GK positioning advance ---
File: aiKeeper.js
The GK should narrow the angle more aggressively. Consider increasing the advance distance by 20-30% for central shots (ball within 15m laterally), so the GK cuts off more of the goal before the shot is taken.

--- 6D: Add shot-blocking by outfield defenders ---
New mechanic (suggested): When a shot is launched, any outfield defender within 3m of the ball trajectory and facing the ball should have a chance to block it (proportional to bravery + positioning attributes). This currently doesn't exist. Real football sees ~25% of shots blocked by outfield defenders before reaching the GK.
  Target: 20-25% of shots should be blocked by defenders.
  Implementation: In the tick after a shot is launched, check each defender within 4m of the ball path. Block chance = (bravery/200 + positioning/200) * proximityFactor. If blocked, deflect ball with reduced speed.

========================================
AREA 7: INCREASE DEAD BALL TIME
========================================

Target: ~32 minutes of dead ball time per 90-minute match (real football: ~42 min, but some is injury/VAR which doesn't apply).

--- 7A: Longer set piece setup ---
File: matchFlowController.js
Current free kick positioning timeout: 8.0s → Suggested: 12.0s
Current other restart positioning: 4.0s → Suggested: 6.0s
Current goal celebration: 5.0-7.0s → Suggested: 8.0-12.0s

--- 7B: Add throw-in delay ---
Current: throw-ins appear to restart quickly
Suggested: add 3-5 second delay for throw-in setup (player retrieves ball, walks to line, waits for teammates)

--- 7C: Add goal kick delay ---
Suggested: 4-6 second delay before goal kick can be taken (GK places ball, surveys options).

========================================
EXPECTED RESULTS AFTER ALL CHANGES
========================================

With all 7 areas applied together:
- Decision rate drops from ~2/sec to ~0.5/sec → ~4x fewer decisions
- Control + cooldown time means each touch takes 3-5s instead of 1.7s
- Defensive interceptions increase ~3x (larger radius + more pressing)
- Shot threshold + accuracy changes filter ~60-70% more shot attempts
- GK + shot blocking saves ~75% of on-target shots
- Dead ball time adds ~25 minutes of non-play

Net effect on shots: ~180-270 per team → ~10-16 per team (matches real football 12-14)
Net effect on goals: ~18-27 per team → ~1-3 per team (matches real football 1.3-1.5)
Expected scoreline range: 0-0 through 5-0, with most matches 1-0 to 3-2

========================================
TUNING PRIORITY ORDER
========================================

If implementing incrementally, apply in this order for maximum impact:
1. AREA 1 (decision cadence) — biggest single lever, ~3-4x reduction alone
2. AREA 5 (shooting difficulty) — thresholds + inaccuracy + team cooldown
3. AREA 3 (defensive effectiveness) — interceptions + tackle range
4. AREA 2 (control/transition times) — slows down action chains
5. AREA 4 (pressing/marking) — closes defensive gaps
6. AREA 6 (goalkeeper) — reduces conversion of shots that get through
7. AREA 7 (dead ball time) — final polish for realism

After each area, run 20+ test matches and compare stats against the calibration targets table. Adjust individual constants up or down based on results.

========================================
QUICK SMOKE TEST
========================================
To quickly validate this approach before implementing all 7 areas, just change these 3 constants and run 10 test matches:
1. Decision interval minimum: 0.50s → 2.0s (playerStateController.js:159)
2. Shot threshold close: 0.72 → 0.85 (aiConstants.js:9)
3. Shot threshold distant: 0.82 → 0.93 (aiConstants.js:10)
This alone should reduce goals from 20+ to ~5-8 per team, confirming the diagnosis.

The RarityStatsBonus table scales ALL stats including injury: Regular→9-22, Epic→51-66, Legendary→77-89. Evidence that injury is a current-injury accumulator (higher=more injured): heal() in upgradesService.js sets targetToBoost.injury=0 (healed=zero injury), records stats[injury]=-oldInjury (negative delta=injury reduced), matchesService.js adds to injury from match stats. Legendary players start with 77-89 injury points — nearly fully injured at creation, requiring a heal card before first use. This contradicts the premium pricing of Legendary cards.
fm2026Files: model.js:693-745 (processRoleDefaultStats — injury in statsToChange at line 720)
legacyDetails: Legacy has no NFT rarity system — not applicable.
gapAnalysis: Pure FM2026 bug introduced as a side-effect of the BUG-005/006 fix (rarity-blind generation). When RarityStatsBonus scaling was added, injury was included in the scaled stats, making high-rarity players start more injured. Not a comparison gap with legacy.
codeSuggestions: Remove injury from statsToChange in processRoleDefaultStats() and initialise it to 0 separately after the rarity loop, so all players start uninjured regardless of rarity. Also review fitness — if fitness is intended as a 'higher=better' stat it may be fine scaled by rarity, but verify intent. Do NOT change the code without developer approval — this is advisory only.

fm2026Details:
6 packs in packs.json. Each pack yields ONLY its own rarity level — no mixed drops.

Pack contents (NFTs minted per pack, includes kits and badges):
  Regular:   0.01 SOL — 1P + 1T + 3B + 1Kit + 1Badge = 7 NFTs
  Rare:      0.05 SOL — 2P + 2T + 2B + 2Kit + 1Badge = 9 NFTs
  Epic:      0.10 SOL — 3P + 3T + 3B + 2Kit + 2Badge = 13 NFTs
  Legendary: 0.25 SOL — 3P + 3T + 3B + 2Kit + 2Badge = 13 NFTs
  Pack 4:    Free test (~100 mixed cards, all rarities)
  Pack 1000: Free hidden rare starter (13 Rare cards)

MINTING COST BREAKDOWN (verified from code):
  Bot tax: 0.001 SOL per candy machine mint (nftService.js:320, hardcoded)
  Per-NFT rent: ~0.00203 SOL (Metaplex mpl-token-metadata v3.4.0 default — NOT explicit in FM2026 code, but standard for account size)
  NFT type: Standard Metaplex NonFungible (nftService.js:303,353) — NOT compressed NFTs (no Bubblegum/Merkle references found)
  Minting signer: SERVER wallet (nftService.js:105-112, createNft uses this.signer) — the platform pays rent, not the user
  User pays: pack price via solPayment guard (nftService.js:402-405) → goes to owner wallet

PLATFORM COST PER PACK (server-side rent):
  Regular:   7 × 0.00203 = 0.0142 SOL rent vs 0.01 SOL revenue = -0.0042 SOL LOSS
  Rare:      9 × 0.00203 = 0.0183 SOL rent vs 0.05 SOL revenue = +0.0317 SOL profit
  Epic:     13 × 0.00203 = 0.0264 SOL rent vs 0.10 SOL revenue = +0.0736 SOL profit
  Legendary:13 × 0.00203 = 0.0264 SOL rent vs 0.25 SOL revenue = +0.2236 SOL profit
  Free:     Cards for Free rarity skip NFT minting entirely (cardsService.js: if rarity != Rarity.Free)

Note: Currently on Devnet (nftService.js:49, Helius RPC). SOL is free on devnet, so losses are theoretical. On mainnet these costs are real.

legacyDetails: n/a — Legacy is not the reference standard for monetisation. See PDF MonetizationTokenomicsSystem for the target design.
gapAnalysis:
PDF proposes 4 tiers: Starter ($2.50, 5 cards), Standard ($7.50, 9 cards), Premium ($25, 16 cards), Ultra ($100, 5 guaranteed high-rarity).

Current FM2026 pricing problems:
1. Regular packs are sold at a LOSS (-0.0042 SOL per pack in server rent costs)
2. Legendary packs are too cheap — 3 packs (~0.75 SOL) gives 9 Legendary players (near full XI)
3. Platform pays all minting rent from server wallet, user only pays pack price
4. Standard Metaplex NFTs used (~0.00203 SOL rent each) — compressed NFTs (cNFTs) would cost ~0.00000008 SOL each (25,000× cheaper) but are not implemented
5. Single-rarity packs remove the excitement of probabilistic drops

On mainnet at SOL ~$140: Regular pack ($1.40) costs ~$2.00 to mint. Platform subsidises every Regular pack buyer.
codeSuggestions:
RECOMMENDED — THREE OPTIONS (combine as needed):

Option A — RAISE PRICES to cover minting costs:
  Starter:  0.05 SOL (7 cards) — covers 0.0142 rent + margin
  Standard: 0.15 SOL (9 cards) — covers 0.0183 rent + margin
  Premium:  0.40 SOL (13 cards) — covers 0.0264 rent + margin
  Ultra:    1.00 SOL (13 cards, high rarity) — premium tier

Option B — LAZY-MINT (RECOMMENDED for free/low-tier packs):
  Generate cards as regular DB records (not NFTs) by default.
  Only mint as NFTs when a player explicitly chooses 'Export to Chain'.
  This eliminates minting costs for cards that may never be traded.
  Export-to-chain fee: 0.01 SOL (covers rent) paid by the USER.
  Free packs already skip minting (rarity == Free check) — extend this to Regular packs.

Option C — SWITCH TO COMPRESSED NFTs (cNFTs):
  Use @metaplex-foundation/bubblegum instead of mpl-token-metadata.
  Cost per cNFT: ~0.00000008 SOL (vs 0.00203 for standard) — 25,000× cheaper.
  Regular pack: 7 × 0.00000008 = 0.0000006 SOL — effectively free.
  Trade-off: cNFTs are stored in Merkle trees, slightly different transfer UX.
  Requires: new dependency, Merkle tree setup (~1.5 SOL one-time for 2^20 tree).

Best approach: Option B for Starter/Standard packs + Option A for Premium/Ultra + Option C as future migration path.

fm2026Details:
upgradesService.js upgrade engine (rewritten 3 Mar):
- Multipliers by booster rarity: Regular 0.05, Rare 0.10, Epic 0.15, Legendary 0.25
- Formula: gain = round(100 × (multiplier / statsCount) × pow(1 - current/100, 0.7)), minimum 1
- Stats boosted per upgrade: Regular 1-2, Rare 2-3, Epic 3-4, Legendary 4-5
- Weighted stat selection: role-priority 3x, catch-up (<40) 2x, resistance (>90) 0.2x
- Sacrifice bonus: (sacrificeRarityMultiplier × 0.8) + (abilityScore/100 × 0.10), adds to upgrade pool

PROBLEM: A Legendary booster on a stat at value 10 gives: round(100 × (0.25/4) × pow(0.9, 0.7)) = round(100 × 0.0625 × 0.93) = round(5.8) = 6 per stat × 4 stats = 24 total stat points from ONE card. That is 24% of the entire 0-100 scale per single card use.

With sacrifice (Legendary sacrifice, ability 80): bonus = 0.25×0.8 + 0.8×0.10 = 0.28. Total pool = 0.25 + 0.28 = 0.53. Per stat at 10: round(100 × (0.53/4) × 0.93) = round(12.3) = 12 per stat × 4 = 48 total. Nearly half the scale in one operation.

A Regular player (avg stats ~20) can be maxed (~95 on key stats) in roughly 15-20 Legendary booster uses = 5-7 packs = 1.25-1.75 SOL ($175-$245). This represents minutes of real time and days of intended gameplay compressed into one session.
fm2026Files: api/services/upgradesService.js (entire file — upgrade(), heal(), sacrifice logic)
legacyDetails: n/a — Legacy upgrade system was also unbalanced. PDF is the reference.
gapAnalysis:
CRITICAL BALANCE ISSUE — the single most impactful problem in the game economy. The PDF designs progression to take months, not minutes. Three compounding factors make FM2026 progression too fast:

1. MULTIPLIERS TOO HIGH: 0.25 for Legendary means each card delivers ~6 points per stat at mid-range values. Even Regular boosters (0.05) are meaningful upgrades.

2. DIMINISHING RETURNS TOO GENTLE: The 0.7 power curve barely resists progression. At stat 50, pow(0.5, 0.7) = 0.41 — still 41% of max boost. At stat 80, pow(0.2, 0.7) = 0.15 — still giving +1-2 per card. The curve needs to be much steeper to create a meaningful endgame grind.

3. TOO MANY STATS PER CARD: Legendary boosting 4-5 stats simultaneously means one card does the work of 4-5. Reducing to 2-3 doubles the cards needed.

Combined effect: current system needs ~20 Legendary boosters to max a player. Proposed system would need ~100+.
codeSuggestions:
RECOMMENDED PARAMETER CHANGES (upgradesService.js):

1. REDUCE MULTIPLIERS (~60% reduction):
   Current:  Regular 0.05, Rare 0.10, Epic 0.15, Legendary 0.25
   Proposed: Regular 0.02, Rare 0.04, Epic 0.08, Legendary 0.12
   Each tier is ~2x the previous (clean progression)

2. STEEPEN DIMINISHING RETURNS:
   Current:  pow(1 - current/maxVal, 0.7)
   Proposed: pow(1 - current/maxVal, 1.5)
   Comparison at key values:
   - Stat 20: 0.7-power = 0.85x → 1.5-power = 0.72x
   - Stat 50: 0.7-power = 0.41x → 1.5-power = 0.18x (less than half)
   - Stat 70: 0.7-power = 0.24x → 1.5-power = 0.05x (nearly zero)
   - Stat 90: 0.7-power = 0.07x → 1.5-power = 0.003x (effectively capped)
   This creates a natural soft-cap around 70-80 where further gains are glacial.

3. REDUCE STATS PER UPGRADE:
   Current:  Regular 1-2, Rare 2-3, Epic 3-4, Legendary 4-5
   Proposed: Regular 1, Rare 1-2, Epic 2, Legendary 2-3

4. NET EFFECT — Legendary booster on stat at 20:
   Current: round(100 × (0.25/4) × pow(0.8, 0.7)) = 5 per stat × 4 = 20 total
   Proposed: round(100 × (0.12/2) × pow(0.8, 1.5)) = round(100 × 0.06 × 0.57) = 3 per stat × 2 = 6 total
   That's 3.3× slower progression — one card now equals ~3-4 days of training instead of 30-60 days.

5. REDUCE SACRIFICE BONUS:
   Current:  rarity × 0.8 (Legendary sacrifice adds 0.20 to pool — almost doubles upgrade)
   Proposed: rarity × 0.3 (Legendary sacrifice adds 0.036 — a small bonus, not a doubler)

fm2026Details:
packs.json content arrays specify a fixed rarity per item. A Rare pack gives ONLY rare cards — there is zero probability of getting an Epic or Legendary from it. The rarity is hardcoded per pack definition, not rolled per card. BoosterType is chosen uniformly random (25% each for Skill/Heal/Scout/Practice in cardsService.js) regardless of pack tier.

This means packs are effectively 'buy exactly what you get' — there is no excitement, no lucky pulls, no reason to buy a lower-tier pack hoping for an upgrade. Every pack purchase is a known-quantity transaction. This fundamentally undermines the pack-opening experience that drives engagement in every successful card game.
fm2026Files: data/packs.json (content arrays with fixed rarity per item)
api/services/cardsService.js (openCardsPack — uses fixed rarity from pack definition)
legacyDetails: n/a — PDF is the reference standard.
gapAnalysis:
The PDF proposes probabilistic drop tables per pack tier, where each card slot has a chance of rolling a higher rarity. This is the industry standard for card games (FIFA Ultimate Team, Pokémon TCG, Hearthstone, etc.) and the primary psychological driver for repeat pack purchases.

Without mixed drops:
- No excitement in pack opening
- No 'chase' for rare pulls
- No stories of lucky pulls to share (viral marketing)
- No reason to buy lower-tier packs at all (if you want Legendaries, just buy Legendary packs)
- Pack revenue is limited to the most expensive tier only

With mixed drops:
- Every pack opening is exciting
- Lower-tier packs have value (small chance of epic/legendary pull)
- Creates the 'one more pack' engagement loop
- Enables pity timer mechanics (gm-004)

fm2026Details: No pity timer or guaranteed rare drop system exists. With the current single-rarity packs this is less relevant, but becomes essential once mixed-rarity drops (gm-003) are implemented. Without bad luck protection, probabilistic drops can lead to very long streaks of low-rarity pulls that feel unfair and drive player disengagement.
legacyDetails: n/a
gapAnalysis:
PDF proposes pity timers guaranteeing rare+ cards every N packs. This is now industry standard — Genshin Impact (90-pull pity), Hearthstone (10-pack legendary pity), FIFA (X packs guaranteed walkout). Without it, players who spend money and get nothing of value churn permanently. Pity timers protect the investment feeling.

Depends on gm-003 (mixed-rarity drops) being implemented first — pity timers only make sense when rarity is probabilistic.

fm2026Details:
Unlimited card generation. Any user can buy unlimited packs of any rarity and generate unlimited Legendary players/trainers/boosters. There are no global or per-rarity supply caps. With current pricing (0.25 SOL per Legendary pack), a single user could generate hundreds of Legendary players for under 10 SOL. This completely undermines NFT scarcity and destroys secondary market value.

The Metaplex candy machine in nftService.js has no itemsAvailable cap set based on rarity — it creates items on demand.
fm2026Files: api/services/nftService.js (candy machine creation — no supply limit)
api/services/cardsService.js (openCardsPack — no supply check before generation)
legacyDetails: n/a
gapAnalysis:
PDF proposes monthly supply caps: 50 Legendaries per month globally. This is THE single most important economic control for an NFT-based game. Without scarcity:
- Legendary NFTs have no secondary market value (infinite supply = zero scarcity premium)
- No incentive to trade on marketplace (just buy more packs)
- No reason to protect or value your cards
- The entire NFT value proposition collapses

Every successful NFT project (NBA Top Shot, Sorare, Gods Unchained) uses strict supply controls. FM2026 currently has none.

fm2026Details:
Sacrifice system exists in upgradesService.js — a card can be sacrificed during an upgrade operation to boost the upgrade pool. Sacrifice bonus formula: (sacrificeRarityMultiplier × 0.8) + (sacrificeAbilityScore/100 × 0.10). The sacrificed card is deleted from DB and NFT is burned on-chain.

Problems:
1. Sacrifice bonus is OVERPOWERED — a Legendary sacrifice (mult 0.25) adds 0.25×0.8 = 0.20 base bonus, which is almost as much as the entire base Legendary upgrade multiplier (0.25). With high ability, the bonus can reach 0.28 — effectively more than doubling the upgrade.
2. Sacrifice is only available DURING an upgrade — no standalone burn-for-value path.
3. No card fusion/combining mechanic (5 Rares → 1 Epic).
4. No dust/currency received from burns.
5. Low-rarity cards have zero value once you have better ones — no sink for unwanted cards.
legacyDetails: n/a
gapAnalysis:
PDF proposes structured burn mechanics: combine cards of same rarity to create higher tier, plus a dust system from burns. Currently FM2026 has sacrifice-during-upgrade only.

The biggest gap is FUSION — the ability to combine 5 cards of the same rarity into 1 card of the next tier. This is critical because:
1. Gives value to unwanted low-rarity cards (they're 1/5 of an upgrade)
2. Creates a 'grind path' for free-to-play players (accumulate + fuse)
3. Acts as a card sink (removing supply, maintaining scarcity)
4. Provides progression that doesn't require pack purchases
5. Works WITH supply caps (fused Legendaries count against monthly cap)
codeSuggestions:
RECOMMENDED CHANGES:

1. REDUCE SACRIFICE BONUS:
   Current:  baseBonus = multiplierByRarity × 0.8
   Proposed: baseBonus = multiplierByRarity × 0.3
   Legendary sacrifice: 0.12 × 0.3 = 0.036 (with proposed multipliers)
   Sacrifice should be a small bonus, not a doubler.

2. ADD FUSION MECHANIC:
   New endpoint: POST /api/fuse
   Body: { cardIds: [5 card IDs], targetType: 'player'|'trainer'|'booster' }
   Rules:
     - All 5 cards must be same rarity
     - All 5 must be same type (player/trainer/booster)
     - Result: 1 card of next tier (random role/stats)
     - 5 Regular → 1 Rare
     - 5 Rare → 1 Epic
     - 5 Epic → 1 Legendary (subject to monthly supply cap)
     - Cannot fuse Legendaries (they're max tier)
   Burns all 5 input NFTs, mints 1 new NFT.
   Minting cost for result card: 0.005 SOL (user pays).

3. ADD DUST SYSTEM (optional, lower priority):
   Burning any card yields dust:
     Regular: 10 dust, Rare: 50, Epic: 200, Legendary: 1000
   Dust can purchase:
     Targeted stat boost: 500 dust = +1 to chosen stat
     Specific booster type: 200 dust = 1 Rare booster of chosen type
     Cosmetic items: 100-500 dust

The club balance field (model.js:334, initialised at 100,000) is stored but never modified — it is purely cosmetic. getSolPriceInUSD() in constants.js is stubbed (returns hardcoded 1.0). There is no play-to-earn reward system, no match rewards, no achievement rewards. Every action in the game is either free or costs SOL — there is no middle ground.

gapAnalysis:
PDF proposes dual tokens: FTBL (utility, earned in-game) and ULTRA (premium, purchased). My recommendation: start with just FTBL as a soft currency (database-tracked, not a blockchain token). Reasons:

1. A soft currency adds friction to actions that are currently free — preventing abuse
2. Creates reward loops: play matches → earn FTBL → spend on training/packs → play more
3. Gives free-to-play players a path to premium content (slow but possible)
4. No need for a second blockchain token (complex, regulatory risk, liquidity issues)
5. The existing club.balance field can be repurposed — it's already there, just unused

A blockchain FTBL token can be added later if needed — the soft currency establishes the economy first.

fm2026Details:
Daily training (trainingService.js): Individual training gives +1-2 points on 1 random stat via getRandomInt(1, 2 + amount) where amount = trainerScore/100 (typically 0.3-0.8, so getRandomInt(1, 2) = always 1-2). Team training gives growthFactor=2 × effectiveness per drill.

Upgrade cards: A single Legendary booster gives +4-12 per stat on 4-5 stats = 20-48 total stat points.

Ratio: 1 Legendary card ≈ 30-60 days of daily training for a single stat. Training is effectively worthless — a player who only uses training would take 2+ years to max a single stat, while a player with Legendary boosters does it in minutes.

This makes the game pay-to-win: the only meaningful progression path is buying packs. Free-to-play training is negligible compared to card upgrades.
legacyDetails: n/a
gapAnalysis:
PDF designs training as the PRIMARY progression path (60% of progression) with cards as an accelerator (30%) and sacrifice/fusion as supplementary (10%). Currently FM2026 has this inverted — cards are 95%+ of progression and training is negligible.

The fix requires both sides: INCREASE training gains AND DECREASE card gains (gm-002). The target ratio should be 1 card ≈ 3-5 days of training, not 30-60 days. This ensures:
1. Free-to-play players progress meaningfully through daily training
2. Card purchases accelerate but don't replace training
3. Daily engagement is rewarded (log in, train, compete)
4. Pay-to-win perception is minimised
codeSuggestions:
RECOMMENDED REBALANCING:

1. INCREASE DAILY TRAINING GAINS:
   Current: getRandomInt(1, 2 + amount) where amount ~ 0.5 → always 1-2
   Proposed: getRandomInt(2, 4 + amount) → gives 2-4 per day
   With a good trainer (score 80): amount = 80/50 = 1.6 → getRandomInt(2, 5) = 2-5 per day

2. SCALE TRAINER QUALITY IMPACT MORE:
   Current: amount = trainerScore / 100 (0.3-0.8 range, barely matters)
   Proposed: amount = trainerScore / 50 (0.6-1.6 range, noticeably better trainers = better gains)
   This also creates incentive to upgrade trainers (currently pointless).

3. WITH PROPOSED CARD NERFS (gm-002):
   Training: ~3 pts/day on 1 stat = ~21 pts/week
   Legendary booster: ~3 pts × 2 stats = ~6 pts per card
   New ratio: 1 card ≈ 3 days training (MUCH more balanced)

4. ADD TRAINING STREAK BONUS:
   If a player trains the same stat 7 consecutive days: +50% bonus on day 7
   e.g., Day 7 gives 3-6 instead of 2-4
   Rewards consistent daily engagement over burst card usage.

5. ADD TRAINING SLOT UNLOCKS:
   Base: 1 training slot (train 1 player per day)
   FTBL purchase: 2nd slot at 500 FTBL, 3rd at 2000 FTBL
   Creates another FTBL sink and rewards engaged players.

fm2026Details:
marketplaceService.js: sell() accepts freeform price parameter with no floor or ceiling validation. Only guard: Free rarity cards cannot be sold. Buy lock period of 5 minutes prevents instant resale. No server-side verification that the actual SOL transaction amount matches the listed sell_price. No marketplace fee or tax on transactions. No price history or suggested pricing.

Vulnerabilities:
1. Sell for 0.0001 SOL to transfer cards between accounts for free
2. No fee = zero friction for wash trading (inflating trade volume)
3. No price verification = buyer can underpay (BUG-010 related: lock date arithmetic is also broken)
4. No SOL amount validation on-chain vs off-chain price

gapAnalysis:
PDF proposes: 5% marketplace fee, minimum price floors, price history, anti-sniping. Current FM2026 marketplace has none of these. The zero-fee freeform-price model enables:
- Free card transfers between accounts (list at 0.0001 SOL)
- Wash trading to inflate trade volume metrics
- Price manipulation (no floor means Legendaries can be listed at 0.001 SOL)
- Loss of platform revenue (every trade should generate fee income)

The marketplace fee is also important as a SOL sink — it removes SOL from circulation and funds platform operations.

fm2026Details:
Club balance = 100,000 (model.js:334), NEVER modified by any game logic. fan_base=10, stadium_capacity=1000 — fields exist on the Club model but are purely decorative. No gate receipts from matches. No player wages. No trainer wages. No sponsorship income. No season prize money. No transfer fees.

Consequence: players can hoard unlimited cards with zero ongoing cost. A squad of 50 Legendary players costs nothing to maintain. There is zero economic pressure to make squad decisions — keep everyone, sell nothing, release nothing. The entire squad management metagame (which players to keep, who to sell, budget management) is missing.
legacyDetails: n/a — Legacy also failed to implement this (TheBoardroom.cs methods were all commented out). The PDF is the reference standard.
gapAnalysis:
THIS IS THE ONLY REMAINING P0 GAP ACROSS THE ENTIRE PROJECT (match engine + game features + monetisation). The PDF proposes comprehensive financial simulation: gate receipts, weekly wages, sponsorship, and prize money. Without financial pressure:

1. No reason to ever sell or release a player (zero maintenance cost)
2. No squad management depth (no budget constraints = no trade-offs)
3. No economic metagame (wages, transfers, stadium upgrades)
4. No penalty for having too many players
5. No reward for good financial management
6. The game is fundamentally missing the 'management' aspect of a football management game

Wages are the CRITICAL piece — they create the only meaningful ongoing cost that forces squad curation decisions.
codeSuggestions:
RECOMMENDED: New file api/services/financeService.js

Revenue sources (calculated weekly, alongside training cron):
  Gate receipts: stadium_capacity × fillRate × ticketPrice
    fillRate = min(1.0, fan_base / stadium_capacity × (1 + leaguePositionBonus))
    ticketPrice = 25 base (scales with league level)
    Example: 1000 seats × 0.8 fill × 25 = 20,000/week
  Sponsorship: fan_base × 0.5 per week (100 fans = 50/week)
  Match win bonus: 500 per league win, 1000 per cup win
  Season prize: 1st=50,000, 2nd=30,000, 3rd=20,000, top half=5,000

Expenses (calculated weekly):
  Player wages: abilityScore × 10 per week per player
    Regular (ability ~30): 300/week
    Legendary (ability ~85): 850/week
    Squad of 25 Legendaries: 21,250/week — EXPENSIVE
  Trainer wages: abilityScore × 5 per week per trainer
  Stadium upkeep: stadium_capacity × 0.1 per week (1000 seats = 100/week)

Processing:
  processWeeklyFinances(club) {
    income = gateReceipts + sponsorship + matchBonuses;
    expenses = playerWages + trainerWages + stadiumUpkeep;
    club.balance += income - expenses;
  }

Consequences:
  If balance < 0: cannot buy packs, cannot list on marketplace
  If balance < -50,000: forced to release highest-paid player
  If balance < -100,000: automatic relegation risk

This creates the core management loop: earn income → pay wages → make squad decisions → earn more

fm2026Details:
In cardsService.js openCardsPack(): BoosterType is chosen uniformly random from Object.values(BoosterType). 4 types: Skill, Heal, Scout, Practice — each has exactly 25% chance regardless of pack tier. A Legendary pack (0.25 SOL) gives 3 Legendary boosters, but there's a 75% chance any given one is NOT a Skill booster (the one used for stat upgrades). Players buying expensive packs often get boosters they don't want or need.

This creates frustration: you buy a Legendary pack specifically to upgrade players, but 2 of your 3 boosters might be Heal or Practice type.
legacyDetails: n/a
gapAnalysis:
PDF proposes weighted booster distribution by pack tier and deterministic booster contents. Two approaches:

1. WEIGHTED RANDOM: Higher-tier packs have higher Skill booster rates
2. DETERMINISTIC (better): Pack contents explicitly state booster types so buyers know what they're getting

The deterministic approach is better for player trust — when spending 1.0 SOL on an Ultra pack, you should know exactly how many Skill boosters you'll get.

Instead of:

fm2026Details:
No cooldown between upgrades. A player can apply unlimited booster cards in rapid succession — there is no time gate, no daily limit, no diminishing returns for repeated use. Combined with the fast progression (gm-002), a player can literally go from Regular rarity to Legendary rarity in a single game session by buying packs and applying boosters back-to-back.

This collapses months of intended gameplay into minutes of wallet transactions.

gapAnalysis:
PDF proposes time-gated progression. Without cooldowns, even with reduced multipliers (gm-002), a whale can still buy 50 packs and apply all boosters immediately. Cooldowns ensure that progression is spread over time, maintaining:
1. Daily engagement (come back tomorrow to upgrade again)
2. Season-length progression arcs (not instant)
3. Value of training (can train daily even when upgrade is on cooldown)
4. Competitive fairness (money can't buy instant teams)

Two approaches: hard cooldown (1/day per player) or diminishing returns (each additional same-day upgrade is less effective). I recommend diminishing returns — it doesn't block whales entirely (they can still spend) but heavily reduces burst value, which is better for revenue while still protecting competitive balance.
